title: philammos subulatus
intro: Philammos subulatus, also known as Indian breadroot, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its taproot and narrow, grass-like leaves. Traditionally, the plant's root has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Philammos subulatus include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a digestive aid. In herbal preparations, the root is often dried and used in teas or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
